summary: Replace cliff overhead penalty with smooth linear formula
context: The CES overhead penalty had an arbitrary cliff at 10% that produced zero penalty below threshold and a steep jump above it, disproportionately affecting Twining (15-25% overhead typical).
rationale: A smooth linear formula (ratio * 100) gives proportional cost at all levels. 10% overhead = 10 penalty points, 20% = 20 points. No cliff, no discontinuity. Marked as provisional pending empirical calibration.

alternatives.option: Keep the cliff formula with adjusted threshold
alternatives.reason_rejected: Any threshold creates an arbitrary discontinuity that distorts comparisons

alternatives.option: Use a sigmoid or exponential curve
alternatives.reason_rejected: Adds complexity without clear benefit; linear is simplest and most transparent
